mechanism_narrative: A persistent downward trend in FEDFUNDS over the trailing 3 months signals monetary easing that lowers the discount rate applied to future equity cash flows, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days on an always-on basis.
failure_condition: Rank IC between FEDFUNDS 3-month trend and forward 20d SPX returns stays below 0.03 over any rolling 2-year window, or positive-trend and negative-trend subsamples show no statistically significant return difference (p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When UNRATE rises by more than 0.3 percentage points over a trailing 3-month window, it signals an inflection toward labor market deterioration that historically precedes equity risk-off repricing, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: In triggered episodes, forward 20d SPX returns are positive more than 55% of the time, or the mean triggered return is not significantly different from the unconditional mean return (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous acceleration in RSAFS (retail sales) and HOUST (housing starts) reflects broad-based consumer and housing demand strength that jointly signals durable economic expansion, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days; neither series alone carries the same signal because retail strength without housing follow-through often reflects transient consumption pulls.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS 3-month annualized growth > 4% AND HOUST 3-month annualized growth > 5%
failure_condition: Joint-trigger episodes fail to outperform single-series trigger episodes by a statistically significant margin (p > 0.05), or Rank IC of the joint signal falls below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: In a late-cycle expansion regime characterized by elevated FEDFUNDS, a deceleration in INDPRO (industrial production) signals supply-side capacity exhaustion and impending margin compression, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days; in early-cycle regimes the same INDPRO deceleration reflects normal restocking and is not predictive.
failure_condition: INDPRO deceleration during high-FEDFUNDS regimes shows no significant negative return skew relative to low-FEDFUNDS regimes (difference-in-means p > 0.05), or Rank IC stays below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window restricted to late-cycle periods.

mechanism_narrative: Once FEDFUNDS crosses above 4.5%, restrictive policy rates begin to bite on short-term liquidity and risk appetite, causing a rapid negative repricing of equities over the following 5 trading days as markets reassess terminal rate expectations.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns following FEDFUNDS crossing above 4.5% are not significantly more negative than the unconditional 5-day return distribution (p > 0.05) across at least two distinct historical episodes.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month acceleration in PAYEMS growth reflects immediate labor demand strength that supports near-term consumer spending expectations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days on an always-on basis.
failure_condition: Rank IC between month-over-month PAYEMS growth and forward 5d SPX returns remains below 0.02 over any rolling 2-year window, or reported PAYEMS revisions reverse sign of the growth signal in more than 30% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: During a monetary tightening regime (FEDFUNDS elevated and rising), a slowdown in GDPC1 growth signals that restrictive policy is successfully cooling aggregate demand toward recession risk, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days; the same GDPC1 slowdown in an easing regime instead reflects policy transmission working as intended and is not predictive of negative returns.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth deceleration during tightening regimes shows no significant negative return bias relative to easing-regime deceleration episodes (difference-in-means p > 0.05), or Rank IC stays below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Rising CPIAUCSL inflation combined with a FEDFUNDS rate that fails to keep pace (i.e., real policy rate falling or staying negative) signals loosening real monetary conditions despite nominal inflation pressure, jointly producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days; neither CPI acceleration nor FEDFUNDS level alone captures this real-rate dynamic.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL YoY growth exceeds FEDFUNDS level (implied negative real policy rate)
failure_condition: Triggered negative-real-rate episodes fail to show significantly higher 20d forward returns than the unconditional baseline (p > 0.05), or Rank IC of the joint real-rate signal falls below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.
